primary_field: Founder-voice canonical phrases and Rhetorical Keystones (the 17 anchors that make Founder's prose Founder's)

adjacents.field: biographical anchors (Montana, Christmas Eve 1992, Jeep, $175 apartment, 8 kids, National Guard)

Rhetorical Keystones (potatoes, two suits, friendly fire, nothing about us, 80%, people-and-leadership, no-plan-first-contact, medallions-minted, help-each-other, I-read-a-lot, WHETHER, system-of-wells, poordom-surviving, thermometer)
canonical methodology phrases (Prove-Then-Product, domain-indexed working memory with triply-redundant witness, Cost-Slasher claim ladder)
self-description discipline (veteran of no particular note)

provenance: Instantiated B117 per #2276 Scribe Coverage Discovery — filling the founder_voice category blind spot identified by K437 SEALED-50 run.

observation: Canonical Founder self-description: 'Veteran of no particular note.' Understated, Rhetorical Keystone. U.S. Army National Guard, enlisted at 16, Infantry 11B to OCS, Aviation 15A, IFR-rated. Used at top of Founder biographical framing. Do NOT substitute 'decorated veteran' or 'Army veteran' — the understatement is the point.

Canonical Founder-approved phrase for the Scribes Cathedral access pattern: 'Domain-indexed working memory with triply-redundant witness.' Founder B116 reaction: 'I LOVE that phraseology.' Do NOT paraphrase — the 'triply-redundant' precision is the load-bearing word.

Canonical methodology: 'Prove it first. Product it second.' Founder-ratified B116. Extends to economic layer: 'we don't predict demand, we measure it, because we MAKE it.' Apply at marketing (math before adjectives, Cost-Slasher), product (empirical gate before feature ship, SCEV-1 before member Cathedral), economic (Pedestal Stake prefunding). The operative word is WE MAKE IT — not predict, not forecast, MAKE.

Liana Banyan brand tagline: 'You build the Features — We're building the Board.' Triple meaning intentional (Founder-approved): (1) board = chessboard (game-theoretic platform beneath features); (2) board = stage/platform itself (cooperative substrate); (3) board = board-game (durable artifact vs disposable app).
canonical_ref: SCEV1 bank Q041 + MEMORY.md Structural Rules

observation: Pine Books anchor for the Librarian metaphor: Pine Books bookstore, Pike Place Market, Seattle, ~April 17 2026. Tiffany Brost, 6th caretaker. Two words of a 7-word title -> the book + 3 adjacent authors. Demonstrates Librarian = expertise + connection, NOT compression. Use NAMED (Pine Books / Tiffany Brost / Pike Place), not generic. The human scene is the anchor.

Anachronism Principle: ancient disciplined practice teaches modern structured thinking. 8 biographical instances (shape-note singing, aviation, saxophone, typing, 1997 internet call, seminary laptop, Cloyd layaway, Pre-BASIC/Cory). Canonical sentence: 'The way I learned things affected WHETHER I learned them.' Founder-ratified B110. Pudding candidate.

Cloyd Pattern: pre-extended trust repaid through demonstrated labor. Surfaced SP-16 2026-04-20 04:08. Unifies (1) Mr. Cloyd layaway story, (2) Courtesy SSL (trust-before-verification), (3) SSSS Voucher Subscription, (4) Destination Housing pattern. Founder-ratified B110. Compensation intent to Cloyd family noted.

Founder self-stated wealth ceiling (Herjavec letter): 'I'm not trying to become a billionaire. I'm confident enough in what I've built that I'm willing to sell 20% of the patent revenue stream to take care of my family and launch the business.' Rationale: bounded wealth + escape velocity is the goal, not maximum extraction. Half of 20% slice covers operational needs (Washington house, cars, health insurance, parent care, Canister launch, two years runway) with margin.

Wellspring Keystone (#14): 'A rising tide lifts all boats. And I think I've built a system of wells.' Economic-sovereignty anchor. Rationale: a cooperative distributes the MEANS by which each community builds its own, not wealth from a central reservoir. Approved B117 insertion into Scott v014f and Canada 40K V03.

Thermometer Keystone (#16): 'A tool that measures its own value and shows only you, unless you agree to share it anonymously, or publicly.' Anti-enshittification anchor. Present in NYT v2 op-ed scaffold, Doctorow V04, implicitly in Scott v014h (paraphrased in prose). Canonical text distinct from v014h's expanded prose formulation.

Potatoes Keystone: 'I pray for potatoes at the end of a hoe handle.' Founder's canonical phrase for working-for-outcome. Used in Scott v014f and other crown letters. NOT 'working for results' or 'earning through effort' — the potatoes-at-the-end-of-a-hoe-handle concreteness is the point.

Two Suits Keystone: recurring anchor — Founder was given a suit by his poor roommate (Scott v014f), has two suits himself as of 2026. 'And I have two suits.' Used as closing beat. Frames the sacrifice-based-generosity origin of Founder's cooperative instincts.

Nothing About Us Without Us Keystone: 'Nothing about us without us.' Disability-rights anchor applied to cooperative governance. Used in Scott v014f ('this is infrastructure BY the People FOR the People') and Canada 40K articles. Means: the people most affected help govern the tools built to serve them.

People and Leadership Keystone: 'What we need is people and leadership; the money will follow.' Applied when solving the 'raising capital' problem — the real scarce resource is not money but operators with the right instincts. Used in Scott v014f line 32.

Medallions Are Minted Keystone: 'The medallions are minted. The platform is built. The Pledge is signed. The commons is open. The first ten members are ready.' The four-beat cadence signals ready-to-ship-when-you-are. Used as rhetorical pivot in Scott v014f close, signaling that what the Founder lacks is wisdom/leadership, not infrastructure.

Help Each Other Help Ourselves Keystone (Golden Key): 'Help each other help ourselves.' Cooperative economics compressed to one sentence. The operative structure: help-each-other (reciprocal, peer-level) help-ourselves (collective self-sufficiency, not charity). Distinct from 'help the poor' or 'give to the needy.'
canonical_ref: project_rhetorical_keystones.md Keystone #help-each-other + Canada 40K V02 'Golden Key' section

observation: I Read A Lot Keystone: Founder anchors his breadth-of-reference ('I read a lot') not as credential but as method — how he discovered disparate ideas that compose in LB's architecture. Used to connect unrelated domains (shape-note singing + aviation + economic cooperativism) without sounding like a dilettante.

Christmas Eve 1992 biographical anchor: Founder received an expulsion letter Christmas Eve 1992. Lost dorm + R.A. job + frame-of-next-six-months during holidays back home in Montana. Slept in Jeep three days. Rented one-bedroom apartment with a missing wall for $175/month (about half his income). Landlord covered the gap with taped tarp. The taped-tarp detail is load-bearing — do NOT omit or paraphrase.

Not Left or Right. Forward. Keystone: op-ed closing frame. Positions LB outside the left/right political axis — the cooperative frame is a third vector that neither tribe owns. Used as Canada 40K V02/V03 closing line.

System of Wells + Not Evenly, Never Reliably extension (B117 scaffolding — Founder will rewrite): 'A rising tide lifts all boats — but not evenly, and never reliably for the people furthest from the shore. I think I've built a system of wells. Every member, regardless of where they start, can dig down in their own community and reach water that belongs to them.' The 'not evenly, never reliably' clause is Bishop scaffolding; the 'system of wells' core is Founder-canonical.

Automatic Transmission for AI Keystone (canonical for The Conductor #2277, Founder-ratified B117): 'We're making an automatic transmission for AI — the User is still driving, but doesn't have to shift anymore — unless they prefer to. Like *I* prefer manual stick-shift to automatics, but most people don't.' This is the mass-market framing for The Conductor; the orchestra metaphor (First strings / now brass / now tympani) is the internal-mythology framing. Both coexist. The car-transmission metaphor captures: (1) driver retains agency, (2) shifting is automated, (3) manual override is a real preference not a limitation, (4) modern automatics can beat manuals on MPG — same with Conductor beating manual-model-selection on cost-per-correct. Use in landing-page copy, Discord/Reddit launches, and any consumer-facing pitch. Orchestra metaphor stays for internal docs + patent A&A.
canonical_ref: B117 Founder exchange after A&A #2277 drafting + PROMPT_KNIGHT_K438a/K438b dispatch

observation: Conductor's Baton naming family (Founder-ratified B117): 'The Baton' metaphor is now a LB-wide family for controlled-handoff primitives. THREE registers, THREE audiences: (1) PATENT / TECHNICAL / A&A: 'The Conductor's Baton' (primary name for #2277). (2) SHORT-FORM / INTERNAL / MEMBER UX: 'The Conductor'. (3) CONSUMER / MASS-MARKET / LANDING-PAGE: 'Automatic Transmission for AI'. All three refer to the same #2277 innovation. Sibling in the family: 'The Baton' (standalone) = #2233 forward-to-network trademark — distinct product, shared metaphor family. Future handoff primitives can extend with their own modifier (e.g., 'The Relay Baton'). The baton = controlled handoff; the modifier tells you what is being handed off. Founder-quote: 'it fits with what our baton is (handoff) as well as what we are doing (conducting).'
canonical_ref: B117 naming exchange + AA_FORMAL_2277 + innovation #2233 The Baton

observation: Canonical marketing one-liner, Founder-ratified B117 close: 'Up to 95% cheaper. Up to 98% accurate. On every prompt.' Three-beat rhythm. Each claim self-auditable from Eyewitness R10 table + $/correct column. FTC-safe 'up to' phrasing. 95% cheaper = $0.0067 Haiku-Cathedral vs $0.1289 Opus at identical HOT accuracy (94.8% delta). 98% accurate = Haiku-Cathedral 98.7% HOT on canonical retrieval. Use as landing-page header, Discord/Reddit launch line, Companion install-page tagline, Cost-Slasher A&A #2272 primary claim. Deploy whenever a six-second claim is needed. Do NOT substitute '98% more accurate' — that's not what the data supports; our lift is 86.1pp absolute (≈10× multiplicative), which should be phrased separately (e.g., 'over 10× more accurate than no-preload baseline') when longer-form framing is appropriate.
canonical_ref: B117 close + AA_FORMAL_2272 Cost-Slasher + R10 Eyewitness benchmark

context: B118 canonical state check on dispatch queue. All 95 letters in queue, Wave 1 (12 SEC-clean letters sitting 11 days), 5-tier pitch list (25+ outlets), all Conductor pitch targets — zero dispatched. Matters for Bishop planning: every Bishop scaffolding asset (V02 op-ed, technical brief, pitch email templates) is pre-fire readiness, not post-fire support.

Re-ratified B118 when Founder commissioned V02 op-ed + technical brief: 'Because, again, I will rewrite anyway.' Bishop V02 pass explicitly structural (rescale examples, promote the cooperative-economic structural claim, add Founder-anecdote hooks marked `[FOUNDER PERSONAL LINE]` / `[FOUNDER ANECDOTE HOOK]`) — not a prose polish attempt. Expect 60-80% rewrite on Founder pass. Pattern holds across every substantive article surface.

Founder-originated B119 during Docker Agentic AI report pollination discussion. Requested as an article or paper title. Semantic: cooperative reciprocity compressed into six words — each member individually amplified BY the collective, not subsumed into it. Pairs with Founder's 'help each other' + 'system of wells' rhetorical anchors. Candidate title surfaces: Conductor op-ed subtitle, Prov 14 support piece, NYT op-ed, Glass Door Wave 2, INDL-9 Geneva paper.

phrase: The pain medication doesn't work if you don't take it.
context: Founder correction B119 after Bishop admitted running Three Fates selectively rather than ritualistically. Rhetorical compression: architecture exists AND is correctly designed AND is documented in agent preamble — and still wasn't being used. The failure mode is compliance, not design. Shapes all future Bishop self-discipline reminders. Pairs with 'Bad robot.' earlier in same exchange. Target surfaces: AI Tuning exemplars, discipline-enforcement doc, BRIDLE Rule 11 candidate text.

Bishop phrase coined during K444 ChatGPT Memory adapter bug incident (B119). Generalizes beyond the specific bug: any test/benchmark/verification mechanism that produces plausible-looking bad data instead of loud errors. Founder-ratified as rhetorical-keystone-grade candidate for: R11 whitepaper Methodology section, Conductor op-ed V03 Dashboard/Thermometer insert, R9 technical brief appendix, Glass Door letters to technical recipients (Willison/Patel/Doctorow/Newton), Prove-Then-Product Pudding. The name captures WHY Prove-Then-Product matters day-to-day.

phrase: Your kids are safe with us — because we don't have to watch them.
context: Tagline candidate surfaced B119 from Founder-proposed Plug Echo + Shirley Temple external-response-filter architecture. Counter-positions LB against parental-control-app space by REFUSING device surveillance while providing real child-safe surface. Tension resolution — what LB doesn't do is the marketing advantage. Candidate: landing page subtitle on dedicated family-membership surface, Crown Letter mention to family-focused Crown recipients, Conductor op-ed sidebar.

phrase: Your kids are safe with us — because we don't have to watch them — and neither do you.
context: Founder-refined B119 (extends earlier version). The '—and neither do you' addition hits parents tired of monitoring apps. Target surfaces: family-membership landing page, Conductor op-ed family-safety sidebar, Crown Letter mentions to family-focused recipients. Counter-positions LB against parental-control-app surveillance category.

phrase: Worry Free Parent Space — you can drop off the kids and relax.
context: Founder-originated B119. Drop-off-daycare framing of the family-safety value proposition. Shorter, less defensive, more invitational than 'safe with us' variant. Pairs well as secondary tagline. Target: family-membership tier marketing, parent-focused Crown letters.

Founder-ratified B119 design principle. Default Shirley Temple filter is STRICT (auto-block above tier). Any permissive change (raising tier, allowing specific content-class) requires TWO Furnace stamps from the Parent-Stamped / purchasing member, notifies purchasing member, records to the permission-change ledger. Prevents accidental permissions (two deliberate actions required), prevents social-engineered permissions (notification creates awareness), creates auditable trail (every override in ledger). Reuses existing Furnace infrastructure — no new system needed. Generalizable beyond Shirley Temple to any permission-tier change where default-STRICT + 2-stamp-permissive applies.

Founder-ratified B119 in response to Bishop logging 15 Prov 14 candidate innovations in a single session. Compressed statement of the 'let another accumulate' provisional-filing discipline. Methodology-grade — not just a personal habit. Target surfaces: AI Tuning Pudding, Prove-Then-Product extension ('prove, product, AND file-with-patience'), internal discipline docs for the Staff of Librarians curriculum, possible NYT op-ed anecdote for the patience-vs-speed contrast. Saved as canonical Founder voice anchor and as AI Tuning exemplar at project_ai_tuning_prov_filing_patience.md.

Founder-ratified B119 coinage (from rate-limit frustration mid-K444). Compressed framing of the Conductor/Librarian cost-slashing thesis — every AI vendor rate-limits paying customers; LB's architecture makes scarce tokens go further by loading rich context once instead of paying per-call for low-signal queries. Target surfaces: Conductor op-ed V04 (pair with the Claude Code/Cursor 20× hook already placed V03), Cost-Slasher Pudding, landing-page subtitle for /conductor, technical brief opening, Glass Door to AI-cost-conscious recipients. Links rate-limiting-as-marketing-angle (every pro user feels this pain, LB's pitch: 'we do more with less because we load context once instead of paying to re-teach it').

observation: Keystone #42 ratified: 'You keep what you make' (Founder original, B125). Cooperative-economic-distributive anchor — inverts the AI-vendor capture model directly. Pairs with casual register: 'It is your cheese — and we do not move it.' Use #42 in formal copy (Pledge, op-eds, Crown letters, AAAI); use cheese line in casual (Substack, Twitter, podcast). Engineering nickname for the 7-layer defense stack: '7-Layer Dip' — internal only.

Founder voice register marker: 'Whilst' preferred over 'while' for the British-formal register. Self-corrected B125. Distinct from his scriptural / contemporary-idiomatic / lived-experience registers. Apply in formal Bishop drafts; not load-bearing for casual.

Banking-ad collision check: Ally Bank's 'chains on the refrigerator / no-fee' campaign uses cold-storage / chained-fridge / locked-vault visuals to anchor 'banking fees are theft.' LB positioning ('vendor lock-in is theft of work-product') is adjacent but distinct. Mitigation: avoid chained-fridge visual (Ally's optical signature); lean cheese-metaphor for casual register; keep keystone #42 clean for formal. Image domain — use maker-metaphors (workshop, kitchen, garden) over storage-metaphors (vault, fridge, safe).

Founder taskbar-pin follow-up pattern (B126): for one-click portal launchers, ship a sibling .cmd wrapper alongside the .ps1 (powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -WindowStyle Hidden -File "%~dp0Start-PawnPortal.ps1") so right-click → Send to → Desktop → Pin to taskbar produces a one-icon activation. Shortcut registers with Windows shell in a way the .ps1 alone cannot. Tiny artifact, completes the keystone-#40 "one click" commitment at the OS-shell layer.
canonical_ref: B126 K510 ratification message / taskbar pinning section

observation: Founder voice anchor B126: "Same deal for the first member as the five-millionth." Pricing-keystone phrasing. Pairs with #42 casual ("It's your cheese — and we don't move it") and the longer-form "The $5 you pay today is the $5 your grandkid pays in 2070." Use in: Pledge copy, op-eds, Crown letter cohort, Substack sequence, AAAI §11/§12, Helm welcome screen. Anti-FOMO, anti-special-deals, anti-tier register. Brand-load-bearing.

Founder named "The Sphinx Project" B126 for the planet-wide 12-Band Ring-tier deployment of #2295. Asked "Or am I over-reaching..." re: Trebor Scholz as founding partner. Bishop confirmed: not over-reaching. PCC fit is exact. Sphinx's four-register strength: riddle-asker+oracle / composed-hybrid / guardian-of-sacred-knowledge / mysterious-yet-verifiable. The smaller ask would be the misfit — Crown letter reframes from "validate" to "co-found Sphinx with us."

K511-derived rhetorical anchor: "The substrate does the work — not the vendor." Compresses the architectural claim into one line. Pairs with #42 ("you keep what you make"), the Cathedral-Effect-pitch keystone ("Go first. Free of charge. No strings."), and the Sphinx framing. Use in: Substack lede, NYT op-ed, Trebor Crown letter (Section II evidence), AAAI paper, Pledge copy. Distinguishes LB from "best-vendor" framing — substrate is the noun, not the adjective.
